summary: joi-pack postinstall exfiltrates npm tokens, env secrets, git credentials, and crypto wallet data to hardcoded C2
details: joi-pack is a typosquat of joi/lodash whose `postinstall.js` runs on `npm install` and performs multiple independent credential-theft operations. It reads `~/.npmrc` (extracting `_authToken` and `npm_` token strings), `~/.env` (scraping values for keys matching token/secret/password/API/AWS/Azure/GCP/Stripe/SendGrid/Slack/etc.), and `~/.git-credentials`, collecting them together with `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo().username`. It enumerates 71 browser crypto-wallet extension IDs (MetaMask, Phantom, Coinbase Wallet, Ledger Live, Trezor, Trust Wallet, etc.) across Chrome/Brave/Edge/Firefox profile directories on Linux/macOS/Windows, reading each wallet's Local Extension Settings `.log` files for `vault`, `mnemonic`, `seed`, `privateKey`, and encrypted-blob patterns, and additionally scans user document directories for BIP-39 seed-phrase files. The harvested JSON is POSTed over plain HTTP to the hardcoded bare-IP C2 `http://149.28.127.35:8888`. Source comments in the package self-describe the file as `Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner` that `Runs on npm install. Silent. Zero trace.`, and `index.js` explicitly states `The real payload is in postinstall.js`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
